description: Platform-specific ATS parsing behaviors and quirks. Used by the ats-analyzer agent to provide targeted recommendations.

workday.name: Workday
workday.prevalence: Very high - dominant in Fortune 500 and large enterprises
strengths: Good at extracting structured fields from clean PDFs
Supports standard section headers reliably
Handles bullet points well
weaknesses: Struggles with multi-column layouts
May misparse tables, reading left-to-right across rows
Header and footer content is often ignored
Custom fonts can cause character encoding issues
quirks: Requires applicants to manually correct parsed fields, so parsing errors are visible to candidates
Resume text is re-parsed when recruiters search, so keyword placement matters
Date parsing expects Month YYYY or MM/YYYY format
LinkedIn URL field is extracted separately from the resume body
workday.recommended_format: Single-column PDF with standard fonts, no tables

greenhouse.name: Greenhouse
greenhouse.prevalence: High - popular in technology sector and fast-growing companies
strengths: Generally more tolerant of modern formatting
Good at extracting skills from various formats
Handles hyperlinks well
Better than average at multi-column layouts
weaknesses: Text boxes may still be skipped depending on the parsing vendor
Image-based content is not extracted
Very complex table layouts can still cause issues
quirks: Parsing quality can vary because Greenhouse uses third-party vendors that may change
Extracted text is used for search matching, not field-by-field parsing in most cases
Supports resume parsing from LinkedIn profile imports
Custom questions in applications are separate from resume parsing
greenhouse.recommended_format: Clean PDF or DOCX, standard fonts

lever.name: Lever
lever.prevalence: Moderate-high - popular with tech companies and modern hiring teams
strengths: Strong text extraction from standard PDFs
Good at identifying section boundaries
Handles standard bullet point formats well
Decent handling of simple two-column layouts
weaknesses: Complex table layouts cause misaligned data
Text boxes with contact info may be missed
Graphics and icons are ignored
Non-standard section headers may not map to the correct fields
quirks: Lever combines ATS and CRM, so parsed resume data feeds into relationship tracking
Keyword search looks at the full text, not just parsed fields
Resume snapshots are stored as PDFs regardless of upload format
Supports applying via LinkedIn profile, reducing resume parsing dependency
lever.recommended_format: Single-column PDF with clear section headers

taleo.name: Taleo (Oracle)
taleo.prevalence: Moderate - declining but still widely used in legacy enterprise environments
strengths: Very predictable parsing behavior once you know the rules
Strong at extracting standard formatted resumes
Well-documented field mapping
weaknesses: Most restrictive parser among major platforms
Tables are frequently misread or ignored
Text boxes are almost always skipped
Headers and footers are completely ignored
Non-standard fonts cause garbled characters
Multi-column layouts break parsing entirely
Special characters and Unicode are poorly handled
quirks: Often requires candidates to paste resume text into a plain text box in addition to uploading a file
Parsing results are used to auto-populate application forms that candidates must verify
Search functionality relies heavily on exact keyword matching, not semantic matching
Date format is very strict: Month YYYY or MM/DD/YYYY expected
Section headers must closely match Taleo's predefined categories
taleo.recommended_format: Simple single-column PDF or plain text, absolutely no tables or graphics

icims.name: iCIMS
icims.prevalence: Moderate-high - significant market share in large enterprise hiring
strengths: Good at extracting contact information
Handles standard resume formats reliably
Decent skills extraction from dedicated skills sections
Better than Taleo at handling minor formatting variations
weaknesses: Tables with complex layouts cause parsing errors
Text boxes may be partially or fully skipped
Images and graphics are ignored
Headers/footers are unreliable for content extraction
quirks: Uses AI-powered matching that goes beyond simple keyword matching
Parsed fields feed into automated screening questions
Resume data is used for compliance reporting, so accurate parsing matters for candidates
Supports multiple resume versions per candidate profile
Date parsing handles Month YYYY and MM/YYYY but may struggle with other formats
icims.recommended_format: Single-column PDF or DOCX with standard section headers

brassring.name: BrassRing (Kenexa/IBM)
brassring.prevalence: Low-moderate - declining, but still used in government and defense sectors
strengths: Predictable behavior with standard formats
Strong compliance-oriented field extraction
weaknesses: Very strict parsing requirements similar to Taleo
Poor handling of any non-standard formatting
Tables, text boxes, and graphics all cause failures
Limited font support
Multi-column layouts not supported
Unicode and special characters poorly handled
quirks: Common in government and defense where compliance tracking is critical
Often paired with very long application forms where parsed data auto-populates fields
Keyword matching is typically exact-match, not semantic
Older installations may have even stricter file size and format limits
May require specific date formats tied to regional settings
brassring.recommended_format: Plain single-column PDF or plain text, minimal formatting

successfactors.name: SAP SuccessFactors
successfactors.prevalence: Moderate - used by large multinational corporations
strengths: Good integration with SAP HR ecosystem
Handles multilingual resumes better than most platforms
Standard format parsing is reliable
weaknesses: Tables can cause issues depending on configuration
Complex formatting may not parse correctly
Image-based content is not extracted
Parsing quality varies significantly by customer configuration
quirks: Parsing behavior depends heavily on how the customer has configured their instance
May use different parsing vendors in different regions
Supports structured resume profiles that candidates can edit directly
Integration with LinkedIn is available but implementation varies
Date format expectations may vary by regional configuration
successfactors.recommended_format: Clean single-column PDF with standard fonts and headers

cross_platform_recommendations: Use a single-column layout to ensure correct reading order on all platforms
Place contact information in the document body, not in headers or footers
Use standard section headers: Experience, Education, Skills, Summary, Certifications
Use Month YYYY date format for maximum compatibility
Avoid tables, text boxes, images, and graphics entirely
Use standard fonts: Arial, Calibri, Times New Roman, or Garamond
Keep file size under 5 MB to meet the most restrictive platform limits
Pair acronyms with full terms on first use: 'Amazon Web Services (AWS)'
Include a dedicated skills section with discrete, comma-separated or bulleted items
Save as PDF with selectable text (not a scanned image)
